business_definition: Stable synthetic customer alias. No customer PII is stored.
usage_and_constraints: Use only as a lookup key; may map to more than one policy.
Retrieve plan terms and coverage matrix using this key.

business_definition: Status as of record_snapshot_date: ACTIVE, EXPIRED, CANCELLED, SUSPENDED.
usage_and_constraints: Do not use alone to assess a historical incident date.

business_definition: Date the protection plan was enrolled.
First covered date under the policy.
Last scheduled covered date under the policy.
usage_and_constraints: Use inclusive date logic for ELG-002.

business_definition: Effective termination date for cancelled policies. Blank otherwise.
usage_and_constraints: Incident date on/after this date is not covered.

business_definition: Start of a suspension period. Blank when not suspended.
usage_and_constraints: Incident date during a suspension period is not active.

business_definition: End of suspension period. Blank means open-ended at snapshot.
usage_and_constraints: Kept for future support of resolved suspensions.

business_definition: Unique internal identifier for the covered device record.
usage_and_constraints: One device per policy in MVP.

business_definition: Synthetic IMEI-like business identifier used for exact matching.
usage_and_constraints: Intentionally non-real; no actual IMEI is used.
For context and narrative validation only.
For context and narrative validation only.
Must be on or before device enrollment date.

business_definition: Date device was linked to the policy.
usage_and_constraints: Aligned with policy enrolment in v1.

business_definition: Date this static synthetic reference data represents.
usage_and_constraints: All policy_status values are as of this date.
